questions.question: What was the landmark in the image original main function?
questions.expected: The Brandenburg Gate originally served as a ceremonial city gate and entrance to Berlin.
questions.rgb_answer: The Brandenburg Gate was originally a triumphal arch, serving as the gateway to the city of Berlin. It was built in 1799-1801 and was one of the largest and most famous gateways in Europe. The gate was designed by architect Johann Gottfried Schadow and was intended to commemorate the Prussian victory over Napoleon's army at the Battle of Jena in 1806. It was also meant to symbolize the unity of the German states under the leadership of King Frederick William III of Prussia.

gt_answers.Small: The Brandenburg Gate was originally a triumphal arch.
gt_answers.Medium: The Brandenburg Gate was originally a triumphal arch, intended to celebrate the victory of King Frederick William I of Prussia over the French in 1760. It was designed by architect Georg Wenzeslaus von Knobelsdorff and sculpted by Johann Gottfried Schadow. The gate was meant to be a symbol of peace and unity, as it was intended to be a gift from the Prussian people to the French people. However, it has since become a symbol of Berlin and Germany, and is one of the most famous landmarks in the world.
gt_answers.Large: The Brandenburg Gate was originally a triumphal arch.

rendered_answers.Medium: The landmark in the image was originally a monument.
rendered_answers.Large: The landmark was originally a triumphal arch.
